article_meta.title: San Francisco sues 10 major food manufacturers over ultra-processe

reasoning: The summary consistently attributes contested claims to the city or industry groups, avoids loaded language, and balances perspectives. It does not adopt the article’s emotive framing (e.g., tobacco comparisons, ‘public health crisis’) without attribution.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs with proper attribution (e.g., ‘alleging,’ ‘accuses,’ ‘arguing’).
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: All contested claims are attributed to the lawsuit or industry groups; factual elements align with the article.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: No subjective judgments stated as fact; evaluative language is clearly sourced.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Avoids emotive phrasing; maintains measured tone.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks or disparagement in the model’s voice.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No assumptions about intentions beyond quoted/attributed allegations.
custom_reasoning.slant: Presents both the city’s allegations and industry rebuttals proportionally.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Includes major perspectives: city, industry associations, and references to scientific/government sources.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: No loaded descriptors are used independently of sources.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Politically and ideologically neutral vocabulary throughout.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Reflects both alleged harms and counterarguments without overemphasis.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Treats official and industry sources even-handedly.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Clearly more neutral than the source; avoids adopting charged framing.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Slight under-detail on specific scientific/government entities, but main sides are represented.
